info.title: Catalogue Item API v1.0
info.description:
## Overview
**CatalogueItem API** is JSON API to enable **creating/retrieving Catalogue items with JSON structured data format**. API offers creating items in `sync` and `async` (batched) operation.

API usage **requires authorization** that can be given by the data pool support team. User's company must have the **Catalogue Items API** configured in the *Company Settings / Services / Additional Services* list. API endpoints are guarded by **throttling** separately for `sync` and `async` calls.

## Changelog

### 3.1.34.0 - 2026.02

* New field is added to `/RequestStatus/Get`: `errorDetails` if the `Status` field is returned with `Failed`.
  The field will contain detailed information about failures that occurred during processing the request.

### 3.1.29 - 2024.10

- Request and Response content types are adjusted to only allow neccessary types for each endpoints
- Deprecated /DraftCatalogueItem endpoint is removed

### 3.1.27 - 2024.04
* `GdsnMessages/Inbound` endpoint updated. The endpoint can also receive XML file as string (content-type should be 'application/xml' or 'text/xml')
* `GrMessages/Inbound` endpoint added to support Global Registry messages via XML content

### 3.1.24 - 2023.10
* `GdsnMessages/Inbound` endpoint added to support Gdsn messages via XML file

### 3.1.24 - 2023.08
* `IncludeGs1Response` parameter in `POST /RequestStatus/Get` is marked Obsolete, and the default is set to true
* `/DraftCatalogueItem/Create` is marked obsolete, `/CatalogueItem/Draft/CreateOne` is introduced with same functionality
* `/DraftCatalogueItem/CreateAsync` is marked obsolete, `/CatalogueItem/Draft/CreateMany` is introduced with same functionality
* `/DraftCatalogueItem/Get` is marked obsolete, `/CatalogueItem/Draft/GetOne` is introduced with same functionality
* `/CatalogueItem/Live/CreateMany` added
* `/CatalogueItem/Live/WithdrawMany` added
* `/CatalogueItemPublication/AddMany` added
* `/CatalogueItemPublication/DeleteMany` added
* `/Message/List` added
* `/Message/GetReviewInformation` added

---
# Status Request

## Request Asynchronous Call Completion Status
### `POST /RequestStatus/Get`
* All asynchronous calls will return a `RequestIdentifier`, and the status of the request can be queried through this endpoint
* Status can be: `NotFound|Waiting|Running|Paused|Cancelled|Failed|Done`
* GS1Response messages are included if the IncludeGs1Response parameter is set to `true`

# Use existing Gdsn standard message
Api supports the use of Gdsn standard XML files and uses validation rules based on the message types

## `POST /GdsnMessages/Inbound`
 * Endpoint receives Base64 encoded `MessageContent` of a standard Gdsn message and returns a `RequestIdentifier`, that can be used to query the completion of the operation
 * An online Base64 encoder like [THIS](https://www.base64encode.org/) can be used to convert XML file content to binary format

# Catalogue Item Creation

## Create Draft Catalogue Items Synchronously

### `POST /CatalogueItem/Draft/CreateOne`
* Items created are `Draft` items so they are **not published**
    * Created Items *can be only queried* with `POST /CatalogueItem/Draft/GetOne` endpoint
    * Created Items are *visible in the Web interface*
* Items created are `public` by default, but `private` can be configured via `DataRecipient` parameter
* Input and Output models are formatted based on `TradeItem XSD Schema`
* Successful calls returns the `GS1Response messages`
* Standard validations are run on calls, such as `CodeList Items` are valid, or `GLN` has proper length
    * Invalid inputs will **not be saved** and they are returned as `GS1Response Message`

### `POST /CatalogueItem/Draft/GetOne`
* Retrieves Draft Catalogue Items created by *aforementioned API* endpoints or on *Web interface*
* Either `Data Source` or `Data Recipient` must provided for `Super Admin` users
* `GTIN` and `Target Market` is mandatory
* Output models are formatted based on `TradeItem XSD Schema`
* Be aware of the `POST` method instead of `GET`, because of the parameters are passed in the `Body`

## Create Draft Catalogue Items Asynchronously

### `POST /CatalogueItem/Draft/CreateMany`
* Endpoint receives an `array of TradeItems`, schedules them for Draft creation, and returns a `RequestIdentifier`, that can be used to query the completion of the operation
* Items created are `Draft` items so they are **not published**
    * Once created, Items *can only be* queried with `POST /CatalogueItem/Draft/GetOne` endpoint
    * Once created, Items are *visible* in the Web interface
* Items created are `public` by default, but `private` can be configured via `DataRecipient` parameter for each item
* Input and Output models are formatted based on `TradeItem XSD Schema`
* Standard validations are run on each item in the array, such as `CodeList Items` are valid, or `GLN` has proper length
    * Invalid items will **not be saved** and they are returned as `GS1Response Message`
    * Valid items are scheduled for the creation

## Create Live Catalogue Item Hierarchies Asynchronously

### `POST /CatalogueItem/Live/CreateMany`
* Endpoint receives an `array of CatalogueItems`. Each instance of the array is considered as a standalone Catalogue Item Hierarchy, and returns a `RequestIdentifier`, that can be used to query the completion of the operation
* `DocumentCommand` parameter instructs the data pool to perform a particular action related to the documents
    * Add – store the Item hierarchies
    * Change - update the existing hierarchies, by total replacement
    * Correct - update the existing hierarchies, by total replacement, skipping certain business specific validation rules
* Hierarchies created are `public` by default, but `private` can be configured via `DataRecipient` parameter for each Item
* The complete GDSN validation set runs on each Item hierarchy in the array
    * Invalid hierarchies will **not be saved** and the reason can be found in the result `GS1Response Message`
    * Valid hierarchies are created as `Live` items
		* Once created, Items *can only be* queried with `GET /SdpCatalogueItem/One/{id}` endpoint located in the *My products API v2.0*
		* Once created, Items are *visible* in the Web interface
* Input model is formatted based on `CatalogueItem XSD Schema`


## Withdraw Live Catalogue Item Hierarchies Asynchronously

### `POST /CatalogueItem/Live/WithdrawMany`
* Endpoint receives an `array of CatalogueItemHierarchicalWithdrawals`, schedules the hierarchies for withdrawal, and returns a `RequestIdentifier`, that can be used to query the completion of the operation
* Removes the hierarchies from the Data Pool, and sends out CatalogueItemHierarchicalWithdrawal message to the subscribers
* Private hierarchy withdrawal can be specified using the `DataRecipients` parameter with the target recipient GLN(s)
* Public hierarchy withdrawal can be specified by using this specific GLN: `5790000500031`
	* All subscribers going to receive the withdrawal message
* Standard validations are run on each withdrawal request
* Input and Output models are formatted based on `CatalogueItemHierarchicalWithdrawal XSD Schema`

# Catalogue Item Publication

## Publish Catalogue Item Hierarchies to Retailers / Target Market Asynchronously

### `POST /CatalogueItemPublication/AddMany`
* Endpoint receives an `array of CatalogueItemPublications`, schedules them for processing, and returns a `RequestIdentifier`, that can be used to query the completion of the operation
* `DataSource` and `TargetMarket` must be specified for every Catalogue Item (GTIN)
* Items are published to the Companies listed in the `PublishToGln` parameter
* Items are published to it's TargetMarket if the `PublishToTargetMarket` is set to the same as `TargetMarket` parameter
* Either `PublishToGln` or `PublishToTargetMarket` must be provided in the request
* May include the `CorrelationId`, which refers to an other API request returned `Request Identifier`. In this case the application waits for the correlated request to be in DONE state.
* Additional recipients in separate requests can be added
* Input and Output models are formatted based on `CatalogueItemPublication XSD Schema`
* Standard validations are run on each Item in the array
* All GDSN validations are run on the catalogueItem to publish
    * Invalid requests will **not trigger any action**, the reason can be found in the returned `GS1Response Message`
    * Valid requests are scheduled for Publication Add

## Remove Catalogue Item Hierarchies from Retailers Asynchronously

### `POST /CatalogueItemPublication/DeleteMany`
* Endpoint receives an `array of CatalogueItemPublications`, schedules them for publication criteria removal, and returns a `RequestIdentifier`, that can be used to query the completion of the operation
* `DataSource` and `TargetMarket` must be specified for every Catalogue Item (GTIN)
* Publication criteria is removed according either the `PublishToGln` parameter or the `PublishToTargetMarket`
* Either `PublishToGln` or `PublishToTargetMarket` must be provided in the request
* May include the `CorrelationId`, which refers to an other API request returned `Request Identifier`. In this case the application waits for the correlated request to be in DONE state.
* CatalogueItem (GTIN) remains live in the data pool regardless of any publication criteria association remained
* Input and Output models are formatted based on `CatalogueItemPublication XSD Schema`
* Standard validations are run on each CatalogueItemPublication Item in the array
    * Invalid requests will not trigger any action, the reason can be found in the returned `GS1Response Message`
	* Valid requests are scheduled for publication removal

## Query Messages Synchronously

### `POST /Message/List`
* Endpoint receives a `Filter, Paging and Ordering` parameter, and returns an `array of Messages` items, that can be used to query the `ReviewInformation`
* Messages can be filtered by `LastUpdatedDate`, filtering for exact match by *Year,Month,Day*
* Messages returned have their `SyncListCode` attached to them
* Default `PageSize` is 100
* Ordering can be any of the `MessageDto` property given in string
* Be aware of the `POST` method instead of `GET`, because of the parameters are passed in the `Body`

### `POST /Message/GetReviewInformation`
* Endpoint receives an `MessageId` returned by `/Message/List`, and returns the `Review Information` for the given Message
* The Result contains the list of the following Review Information
    * `ConfirmationStatusCode`
    * `ConfirmationStatusCodeDescription`
    * `AdditionalConfirmationStatusLongDescription`
    * `CorrectiveAction`
* Be aware of the `POST` method instead of `GET`, because of the parameters are passed in the `Body`
---
## Limitations
* Internal Media Items can’t be added to Draft Items neither via this new API nor via Media API
* Maximum Item count per async create request is 1000

---
## Authentication
You can choose between two methods once SSO is enabled:
* Cookie authentication - Old way (Still supported)
  * The API uses simple cookie authentication. A successful `Account/LogIn` method call sets the `.ASPXAUTH` cookie, which should be sent with all further requests.
  * The token provided in this cookie can be used for 48 hours, so it is intended to be saved for later calls.
  * You can use the old way login mode ( cookie-based ).
  * It is available regardless of the SSO configuration

* Token authentication  - New way
  * Call the Token endpoint with your user name and password
  * Save the response access_token
  * When you call the Api endpoints: Add the saved access token to the Authorization header.
  * Example: Authorization=Bearer access_token
  * This can also be used in Swagger as an api_key
  * Note: This endpoint is only available when SSO is turned on, otherwise use the cookie based authentication!
  * Note: Logging in to GDSN via SSO will no longer save a cookie to the browser, so you will need to log in manually in Swagger to acquire the token when SSO is enabled

# The cookie authentication is retiring, we will only support it for a short period of time.

It is totally unnecessary to log in before and log out after every call as this invalidates the token that would otherwise be active for a lot longer.
We insist upon saving this token and using it for the given duration.

## Errors

The API uses standard HTTP status codes to indicate the success or failure of the API call.

## Accessibility information
## API Throttling

* The API can serve a **limited number of request** within a given time period
* The limits are lower within working hours and higher outside working hours
* The API is **unavailable** during system database maintenance periods (usually during Saturday night)



## Limits within working hours (on weekdays 07:00-18:00 local-time)
* Url patterns: *
* Request limit per second: 5
* Request limit per minute: 60
* Request limit per hour: 500
* Request limit per day: 5000


## Limits outside working hours
* Url patterns: *
* Request limit per second: 10
* Request limit per minute: 120
* Request limit per hour: 1000
* Request limit per day: 10000


## Limits within working hours (on weekdays 07:00-18:00 local-time)
* Url patterns: /DraftCatalogueItem/Create, /DraftCatalogueItem/CreateAsync, /Draft/CreateOne, /Draft/CreateMany, /Live/CreateMany, /Live/WithdrawMany, /CatalogueItemPublication/AddMany, /CatalogueItemPublication/DeleteMany, /GdsnMessages/Inbound
* Request limit per second: 1
* Request limit per minute: 60
* Request limit per hour: 500
* Request limit per day: 5000


## Limits outside working hours
* Url patterns: /DraftCatalogueItem/Create, /DraftCatalogueItem/CreateAsync, /Draft/CreateOne, /Draft/CreateMany, /Live/CreateMany, /Live/WithdrawMany, /CatalogueItemPublication/AddMany, /CatalogueItemPublication/DeleteMany, /GdsnMessages/Inbound
* Request limit per second: 1
* Request limit per minute: 60
* Request limit per hour: 500
* Request limit per day: 5000

post.description: Response will contain the Bearer access token, which should be sent with all further requests as an Authorization Header. You can use this for the api_key with Bearer prefix in Swagger
Response will contain the .ASPXAUTH cookie, which should be sent with all further requests. This endpoint will retire, but will still be supported for a while, once SSO is enabled.

404.description: Company was specified but not found.

post.summary: Switch between companies if the user has more than one .
post.description: Response will contain the new .ASPXAUTH cookie, which should be sent with all further requests. This endpoint will retire, but will still be supported for a while, once SSO is enabled. However, this endpoint cannot be used with token authentication, if you need to switch company call 'Token' endpoint with required company GLN.

parameters.name: gln
parameters.description: The GLN of the selected company.

post.description: Log Out from the api, clears the the auth cookie.

post.summary: Creates a new Draft Catalogue Item Save request synchronously.
post.description: Creates a new Draft Catalogue Item Save request, and the Item is saved synchronously.

200.description: OK or Gs1ResponseMessage with error message

post.summary: Creates a list of Draft Catalogue Item Save requests asynchronously.
post.description: Creates a list of Draft Catalogue Item Save requests, which will create the draft items asynchronously. The endpoint returns a Request identifier, which status' can be queried with the RequestStatus endpoint

200.description: OK or Gs1ResponseMessage with error message

post.summary: Get a Draft Catalogue Item by Key.
Creates a list of Catalogue Item Hierarchy Creation requests asynchronously.
post.description: Creates a list of Catalogue Item Hierarchy Creation requests, which will create the hiearchies asynchronously. The method does not create a Draft Item if the validation fails. The endpoint returns a Request identifier, which status' can be queried with the RequestStatus endpoint.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: CreateManyRequest - Contains the full Catalogue Items with hierarchy structures.

200.description: CreateManyResponse - OK or Gs1ResponseMessage with error message

post.summary: Creates a list of Withdraw Catalogue Item requests asynchronously.
post.description: Creates a list of Withdraw Catalogue Item requests. The endpoint returns a Request identifier, which status' can be queried with the RequestStatus endpoint.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: WithdrawManyRequest - Contains the full Catalogue Items with hierarchy structures

200.description: WithdrawManyResponse - OK or Gs1ResponseMessage with error message

post.summary: Creates a list of Catalogue Item Publication Add requests asynchronously.
post.description: Creates a list of Catalogue Item Publication Add requests, which will schedule them for processing in the hierarchy to the selected retailers asynchronously. The endpoint returns a Request identifier, which status' can be queried with the RequestStatus endpoint

parameters.name: request
parameters.description:
AddManyRequest - Contains the Catalogue Item References, and the publication properties: Glns or TargetMarket
           May include the Correlation ID, which refers to an other API request returned Request Identifier. In this case the application waits for the correlated request to be in DONE state.

200.description: AddManyResponse - OK or Gs1ResponseMessage with error message

post.summary: Creates a list of Catalogue Item Publication Delete requests asynchronously.
post.description: Creates a list of Catalogue Item Publication Delete requests, which will schedule them for publication criteria removal from the selected retailers asynchronously. The endpoint returns a Request identifier, which status' can be queried with the RequestStatus endpoint.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description:
DeleteManyRequest - Contains the Catalogue Item References, and the publication properties: Glns or TargetMarket
           May include the Correlation ID, which refers to an other API request returned Request Identifier. In this case the application waits for the correlated request to be in DONE state.

200.description: DeleteManyResponse - OK or Gs1ResponseMessage with error message

post.summary: Creates a new Gdsn standard message save request asynchronously.
post.description:
Endpoint receive object parameter which has a different meaning for each content type
'text/xml' or 'application/xml' - Endpoint receives XML as string of a standard Gdsn message and returns a `RequestIdentifier`,
that can be used to query the completion of the operation
'text/json' or 'application/json' - Endpoint receives Base64 encoded `MessageContent` of a standard Gdsn message and returns a `RequestIdentifier`,
that can be used to query the completion of the operation
Example by content type:
text/xml:
"<catalogue_item_notification:catalogueItemNotificationMessage p1:schemaLocation="urn:gs1:gdsn:catalogue_item_notification:xsd:3 http://www.gdsregistry.org/3.1/schemas/gs1/gdsn/CatalogueItemNotification.xsd" xmlns:p1="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns:catalogue_item_notification="urn:gs1:gdsn:catalogue_item_notification:xsd:3">
...
</catalogue_item_notification:catalogueItemNotificationMessage>"
            
text/json:
"
{
  "MessageContent": ""
}
"

post.summary:
Creates a new Gdsn standard message save request asynchronously.
Message is accepted only from GlobalRegistry
post.description:
Endpoint receive object parameter which has a different meaning for each content type
'text/xml' or 'application/xml' - Endpoint receives XML as string of a standard Gdsn message and returns a `RequestIdentifier`,
that can be used to query the completion of the operation
Example by content type:
text/xml:
"<catalogue_item_notification:catalogueItemNotificationMessage p1:schemaLocation="urn:gs1:gdsn:catalogue_item_notification:xsd:3 http://www.gdsregistry.org/3.1/schemas/gs1/gdsn/CatalogueItemNotification.xsd" xmlns:p1="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns:catalogue_item_notification="urn:gs1:gdsn:catalogue_item_notification:xsd:3">
...
</catalogue_item_notification:catalogueItemNotificationMessage>"

post.summary: Get list of all the Messages.
post.description:
List all the Messages filtered by the LastUpdatedDate parameter. Further information can be queried with the GetReviewInformation endpoint about each Item.
Super admins can use the dataSource filter. Also note, that the lastUpdatedDate cannot be older than one year.

post.summary: Get Review Information based on the Message Id.
Get API request status based on the request identifier.

GdsnTradeItemClassification.GpcCategoryDefinition: Includes any products that can be described/observed as any variety of frozen fruit or combination of fruits, which may be whole or stoned, pitted, chopped, cored and/or peeled, but has not gone through any further manufacturing processes, such as reformed or cooked, however these products can be coated, in sauce, stuffed or filled. These products must be frozen to extend their consumable life. Specifically excludes tomatoes. Excludes products such as Prepared and Processed Fruit and Fresh and Shelf Stable Unprepared and Unprocessed Fruits.

Includes any products that can be described/observed as any variety of frozen fruit or combination of fruits, which may be whole or stoned, pitted, chopped, cored and/or peeled, but has not gone through any further manufacturing processes, such as reformed or cooked, however these products can be coated, in sauce, stuffed or filled. These products must be frozen to extend their consumable life. Specifically excludes tomatoes. Excludes products such as Prepared and Processed Fruit and Fresh and Shelf Stable Unprepared and Unprocessed Fruits.
